presentation_options.text: What is different in meaning between “mem” and “mem”? What does the extra part do?
Starting from “mem” (meaning ‘mem’), how do you say it when it is a different person, number, or tense?
If we change the subject from “mem” to “mem”, does “mem” change? How?
Inside “mem”, is “mem” a piece you can take off and still have a word, or does it only ever appear stuck on?
Is there a difference between “mem” and “mem”? Do they mean different things, or are they the same word said two ways?
Can you say “mem”? Does it sound right / natural to you?
Which of these sounds most natural: ‘mem’? Are any of them wrong?
